content:
# Shu'aiba Formation

**Summary**: The uppermost formation of the Thamama Group.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] characterized the shuaiba-formation as a shallow shelf carbonate.

- [[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]] analyzed the shuaiba-formation for wellbore stability.

- [[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024]] analyzed the Shuaiba formation at a depth of 909 meters.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] shallow shelf carbonate

- [[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]] analyzed the shuaiba-formation for wellbore stability.

- [[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024]] analyzed the Shuaiba formation at a depth of 909 meters.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] not stated in source

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Contains orbitolinid foraminifera and rudists; average thickness is not specified but it is the top unit. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 48]])

- Included as one of the primary lithologies for rock property correlation and stress modeling. ([[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014, p. 45]])

- The maximum offset to avoid phase change for Shuaiba was determined to be 2,235 m. ([[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024, p. 58]])
